items.text: FollowUpAction struct in norn::tool::follow_up with action (name), description, tool (target tool name), args (pre-populated argument overrides), expires (ExpiryCondition), confidence, and before_content (BeforeContentSource) fields
ExpiryCondition enum in norn::tool::follow_up with FileModified(PathBuf), AnyFileModified(Vec<PathBuf>), TurnScoped, and Never variants
BeforeContentSource enum in norn::tool::follow_up with YggdrasilOp(OperationId), StoredContent(HashMap<PathBuf, String>), and Unavailable variants
FollowUpAction serializes to JSON matching the follow_ups array format in tool results (action, description, expires fields visible to model)
follow_up tool registered with tool_call_id (string) and action (string) parameters
Tool looks up tool_call_id in the action log and returns a structured error if not found
Tool finds the named action in the call's registered follow-up actions and returns a structured error if no such action exists
Tool checks expiry conditions before executing and returns error with reason and suggestion if expired
Tool retrieves original tool call's arguments from the event store
Tool applies the follow-up's argument overrides to the original arguments (override replaces specific fields, all others from original)
Tool invokes the target tool with the merged arguments
Result returned as if the target tool had been called directly — same output format, same structured fields
edit tool registers an undo follow-up action on successful commit that restores the file to pre-edit content
write tool registers an undo follow-up action on successful commit that restores the file to pre-write content
apply_patch registers an all-or-nothing undo follow-up that reverts all patched files
apply_patch registers per-file undo follow-ups (undo_file:<path>) for each file in the patch
All-or-nothing undo uses Yggdrasil operation tracking (revert_operation) when available, falls back to stored content otherwise
Per-file undo uses stored before-content directly (not operation revert, which is all-or-nothing)
Undo follow-up expires with file_modified:<path> (single file) or any_file_modified (multi-file all-or-nothing)
FileModified expiry checks whether the specified file has been modified since the original call via content hash comparison
AnyFileModified expiry checks whether any file in the set has been modified since the original call
TurnScoped expiry marks the action invalid at end of the current turn
Never expiry means the action does not expire
Expiry checked at execution time, not storage time — cheap hash or timestamp comparison
Expired follow-up returns structured error with the reason (which file changed), the tool_call_id, the action name, and a suggestion to re-read and re-generate
Follow-up actions store argument overrides, not complete argument sets
Merging is deterministic: override replaces named fields, all other fields come from the original call
Large arguments (patch text, file content, old_string) read from the action log's stored copy of the original call — never re-transmitted through the model
apply_patch registers apply_structural and apply_auto follow-ups on strict-mode failure
apply_patch registers apply_dry_run follow-up when a dry-run completes successfully
edit registers apply_at_occurrence_N follow-ups on ambiguous multi-match
write registers undo follow-up on successful commit
bash registers rerun and rerun_with_timeout follow-ups
Follow-up actions in tool results include the follow_ups array visible to the model with action, description, and expires fields
Tool trait gains a register_follow_ups method as a new lifecycle phase after on-success
register_follow_ups also runs after error for failure-recovery follow-ups (e.g., apply_structural on strict failure)
register_follow_ups returns Vec<FollowUpAction>
Registry attaches returned follow-ups to the tool result under a follow_ups key
Phase is optional — default implementation returns empty Vec, tools without follow-ups are unchanged
Mutation tools record changes as Yggdrasil operations with operation_id mapping to tool_call_id, files modified with before/after content hashes, timestamp, and agent identity
YggdrasilOp variant on BeforeContentSource is preferred when Yggdrasil tracking is active
StoredContent variant captured eagerly at register_follow_ups time from content the tool read during execute()
Unavailable variant returns a clear error on undo attempt — no silent failure
libyggd::ops::revert_operation(operation_id) atomically restores all files to pre-operation state
When Yggdrasil tracking is not available (norn used outside a Yggdrasil repo), undo falls back to writing stored content from the action log
follow_up tool validates tool_call_id exists, action is registered, and action has not expired before executing — invalid or expired references produce clear errors (CO1)
Argument override merging is deterministic — override replaces specific fields, all other fields from original call, no deep-merge ambiguity (CO2)
Target tool's full lifecycle runs when invoked via follow-up: pre-validate, execute, post-validate, on-success, register-follow-ups — no shortcut that bypasses validation (CO3)
Follow-up execution logged in action log as a new entry with a reference to the original tool_call_id — action log shows the chain (CO4)
follow_up tool itself does not produce follow-up actions — no recursive follow-ups; target tool's result may produce new ones attributed to the target (CO5)
Yggdrasil operation tracking is optional — undo falls back to stored content when libyggd unavailable or directory is not a Yggdrasil repo (CO6)
ToolError::PostValidationFailed carries optional committed_output so follow-up actions on failed-but-committed mutations can reference the structured output (PR0)
PostCheckResult type returned from RuntimePostValidateCheck::check() with outcome and advisories fields, enabling advisory follow-up registration (PR1)
